usage_note: Ruiz et al. paper discretizes interarrival times into 15-minute periods across a full day, capturing morning/lunch/evening peaks at airport security.
Two primary passenger classes: regular and PreCheck. PreCheck passengers route to dedicated lanes with faster service.
Same as customer_classes at the entity level — the model tracks class as an attribute throughout the flow.
TSOs and TDCs operate on shift schedules modeled explicitly in the SIMIO production variant.
After primary screening, a fraction of passengers are routed to secondary inspection based on a Bernoulli outcome from scan alarm rates.
The reactive and proactive staffing strategies in the Ruiz paper dynamically route staff based on current or predicted cycle time — a form of conditional routing of resources rather than entities.
Within a lane class, passengers may use shortest-queue routing in the simplified GT ISYE 6501 teaching variant.
